item.title: Trump Set to Sign AI Cybersecurity Directive as Soon as Thursday
item.description: President Donald Trump is poised to issue an executive order as soon as Thursday aimed at bolstering artificial intelligence cybersecurity and has asked tech industry leaders to join for the event, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Why South Korea’s Won Is Weak Despite An AI-Driven Stock Boom
item.description: A global AI boom has turbocharged demand for chips from South Korea’s tech giants, including SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., helping to drive the country’s benchmark Kospi stock index up more than 150% over the past year. Yet despite the Kospi outpacing every other major equity market in the world, the South Korean won remains one of Asia’s weakest currencies.

item.title: Singtel’s Net Income Rises 40% With Annual Dividend at Highest
item.description: Singapore Telecommunications Ltd., the country’s largest telecom operator, reported a full-year net profit of S$5.61 billion ($4.4 billion), lifted by gains from stake sales in Bharti Airtel Ltd.

item.title: Australian Rare Earths Miner Gives Go-Ahead for Massive Project
item.description: Arafura Rare Earths Ltd. has made a final investment decision to develop its massive Nolans mine in the Northern Territory of Australia, which is set to produce about 5% of global rare earths.

item.title: Canadian Fund Firm Mackenzie Cuts Portfolio Managers to Trim Costs
item.description: Canadian fund manager Mackenzie Investments has laid off a number of portfolio managers as part of a broader restructuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: S. Korea’s Early Trade Data Reflect Sustained Semiconductor Boom
item.description: South Korea’s export growth remained robust in the first 20 days of May, signaling resilient external demand at a time when higher oil prices and broader inflation concerns are complicating the outlook for policymakers.

item.title: Citadel Securities Added 60 People in Asia, Half in Hong Kong
item.description: Citadel Securities LLC hired more than 60 people across six markets in Asia this year, with nearly half in Hong Kong, as Ken Griffin bolsters the market maker’s footprint in the region.

item.title: Japan Maintains Trade Surplus as Energy Imports Plunge
item.description: Japan’s trade balance unexpectedly stayed in the black for a third consecutive month on strong exports and a sharp drop in energy imports.

item.title: RBC BlueBay Adds to Yen Longs on Intervention, BOJ Rate View
item.description: RBC BlueBay Asset Management added to long yen positions this week as the currency drifted back toward 160 per dollar, viewing the level as increasingly attractive amid possible intervention and expectations of a Bank of Japan rate hike in June.

item.title: BOJ to Sound Out Market on Bond Purchase Cuts as Yields Surge
item.description: The Bank of Japan will consult market participants on the pace of tapering its bond purchases this week as yields soar to multi-year highs on concerns over spending and inflation.

item.title: Mounting Exporter Strain Risks Complicating China’s Yuan Policy
item.description: The yuan’s continued strength is exerting pressure on Chinese exporters on a scale rarely seen in recent years, complicating Beijing’s efforts to manage the pace of currency gains.

item.title: Baillie Gifford, Layer Global Add $200 Million to Kalshi Funding
item.description: Kalshi Inc. raised an additional $200 million from two new investors, broadening a funding round that had already brought in $1 billion, according to a person familiar with the situation.

item.title: AvalonBay, Equity Residential Said to Near Mega-REIT Tie-Up
item.description: AvalonBay Communities Inc. and Equity Residential are nearing a deal that will see two of the biggest real estate investment trusts by value combine, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Raizen Races to Win Creditor Support as Bondholders Hold Out
item.description: Raizen SA is considering pushing ahead with a debt restructuring plan over the objections of offshore bondholders, calculating that it has enough backing from bank creditors and local noteholders to win majority support, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Hopes of US-Iran Truce Lower Odds of Rate Hikes
item.description: Gold was little changed as optimism over efforts to end the Middle East conflict eased bets on interest-rate hikes.

item.title: SpaceX to Reward Musk for Mars Colony With 1 Million People
item.description: SpaceX Chief Executive Officer Elon Musk can boost his overall compensation if the company succeeds in building colonies on Mars and data centers in space, according to a US Securities and Exchange Commission filing on Wednesday.

item.title: SoftBank-Backed Firm to File for IPO on Data Center Ambition
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp.-backed digital infrastructure firm SB Energy Corp. plans to file a confidential draft registration statement for a proposed initial public offering in the US, the company said in a Wednesday statement.

item.title: OpenAI Faces IPO Unknowns Even After Victory Over Elon Musk
item.description: Elon Musk’s legal battle against OpenAI has hung over the ChatGPT maker for more than two years, threatening to unravel its restructuring as a for-profit business and undermine its plans to go public.Now that a judge has dismissed the billionaire’s case, OpenAI appears intent on moving ahead with its Wall Street debut. The company aims to file for an initial public offering in the coming days or weeks, according to a person familiar with the matter, with the goal of going public in the fall.The

item.title: Musk Ally Gracias Stands to Make Billions Off SpaceX IPO
item.description: Antonio Gracias, the founder of Valor Equity Partners and a longtime Elon Musk ally, controls a 7.3% stake in SpaceX, making him the second-largest holder after Musk himself that will earn the investor a giant windfall.

item.title: Oil Edges Up After Plunging on Optimism Over US-Iran Agreement
item.description: Oil inched higher after plummeting on Wednesday, as President Donald Trump said the US is in the “final stages” with Iran.

item.title: OpenAI Faces IPO Unknowns Even After Victory Over Musk
item.description: The ChatGPT maker is pushing ahead with a public offering after a judge dismissed Musk’s case against it

item.title: Bridging Finance Founder David Sharpe Seeks New Appeal in Fraud Case
item.description: The former chief executive officer of private credit shop Bridging Finance Inc. will ask Ontario’s highest court to review a fresh ruling that upheld fraud findings against him.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Rise as Nvidia Whipsaws on Results: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian equities were set to climb Thursday after gains on Wall Street that reflected a constructive outlook for US-Iran talks. Nvidia Corp. shares whipsawed in a lukewarm response to rising data center sales.

item.title: Samsung, TSMC Spur Reality Check After Historic AI Stock Boom
item.description: Samsung and TSMC are leading a historic AI stock boom reshaping Korea and Taiwan. It's also raising questions about who benefits.

item.title: Anthropic to Pay SpaceX Nearly $45 Billion for Computing Deal
item.description: Anthropic PBC has agreed to pay Elon Musk’s SpaceX nearly $45 billion over the next three years for computing resources as part of an expanded deal to support its Claude artificial intelligence software, according to a securities filing.

item.title: SEC Chief Atkins Pumps the Brakes on Prediction-Market Funds
item.description: The top US securities regulator is delaying the launch of a wave of novel exchange-traded funds that would let investors wager on events such as elections and economic data as the agency weighs how far the $15 trillion ETF wrapper can stretch.

item.title: Reeves to Set Out UK Cost of Living Plan to Cushion Iran War Hit
item.description: Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves will detail a package of measures to ease cost-of-living pressures on Thursday, as the British government tries to limit the impact of the Iran war.

item.title: Codelco Eyes $2 Billion Gain From Unifying Copper Mines in Chile
item.description: Codelco is seeking a combined $2 billion in cost savings and additional revenue by integrating the operations of three copper mines as the state-owned Chilean company tries to offset the impact of stagnating output and rising debt.

item.title: Anthropic on Pace for First Profitable Quarter as Revenue Surges
item.description: Anthropic PBC is on pace for its first profitable quarter after experiencing a surge in revenue driven by demand for its artificial intelligence software.

item.title: SpaceX Has Spent More Than $15 Billion on New Starship Rocket
item.description: SpaceX said it has sunk more than $15 billion into developing its massive Starship rocket, which is manufactured and launched out of SpaceX’s Starbase facility in South Texas.
